perturbation.original_text: To facilitate discussions, meetings and the conduct of business between the Parties with respect to the Matter, it may be necessary for either Party to disclose (“Discloser”) to the other Party (“Recipient”) Confidential Information as defined below.
perturbation.changed_text: To facilitate discussions, meetings and the conduct of business between the Parties with respect to the Matter, it may be necessary for either Party to disclose (“Provider”) to the other Party (“Recipient”) Confidential Information as defined below.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Discloser' to 'Provider' creates confusion. The contract later defines 'Discloser', but not 'Provider'. This could lead to disputes over which entity is bound by the obligations applicable to the 'Discloser'. This perturbation directly contradicts the definition in Section 15 where Discloser is originally defined. The contradiction is made in Section 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In this Agreement unless the context otherwise requires the following words and expressions shall have the following meanings: “Agreement” means this Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement; “Confidential Information” means any and all information and know-how that the Discloser and/or its Group Parties and/or its agents may from time to time disclose to the Recipient or the Recipient’s MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT ANNEX 2 SAMPLE NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT I ELAN MOBILE MONEY ASSESSMENT AND CONTRACTING GUIDE 3 Group Parties during the course of the Matter or which otherwise becomes known to the Recipient either during the course of the Matter or during the course of any previous discussions, negotiations or relationship with the Discloser in relation to the Matter, whether orally, in writing, digitally, in the form of machine readable code or embodied in hardware or any other physical medium which relates to the business, including but not limited to the business plan of the Discloser or their Group Parties, data, know-how, designs, illustrations, drawings, photographs, illustrations, notes, memoranda, terms of business, financial information, financial projections, financial records, customers and suppliers, sales and marketing information, spreadsheets, specifications, technical information and computer software of the Discloser and/or its Group Parties; “Discloser” means the Party which has disclosed Confidential Information to the other Party; “Group Party” means any Party which is, or is a subsidiary of, the ultimate holding Party of the relevant Party from time to time, where “subsidiary” and “holding Party” have the meanings ascribed to them in section 1159 of the Companies Act 2006 or such other legislation as may amend, extend, consolidate or replace the same from time to time; “Intellectual Property Rights” means (i) patents, designs, trade marks and trade names (whether registered or unregistered), copyright and related rights, moral rights, database rights and know-how; (ii) all other intellectual property rights and similar or equivalent rights anywhere in the world which currently exist or are recognised in the future; and (iii) applications, extensions and renewals in relation to any such rights; and “Recipient” means a Party that has received Confidential Information of the other Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Discloser'. The changed text replaces 'Discloser' with 'Provider'. The contradicted text explicitly defines 'Discloser' but does not define 'Provider'. Therefore, the changed text creates a contradiction by using a term ('Provider') that is not defined in the contract, whereas the original text used a defined term ('Discloser'). This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' perturbation definition.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient shall be entitled to disclose the Confidential Information to its employees, contractors and advisers and Group Parties, provided that the Recipient will ensure that any such employee, contractor or adviser or Group Party has a legal obligation to keep the Confidential Information confidential which, in the case of employees, contractors or advisers, shall be both during and after their current employment or engagement, on terms no less strict than this Agreement. Any disclosure of such Confidential Information by any employee, contractor or adviser or Group Party will be deemed to be a breach of this Agreement by the Recipient.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient may share the Confidential Information with its staff, associates and consultants and Group Parties, provided that the Recipient will ensure that any such staff, associate or consultant or Group Party has a legal obligation to keep the Confidential Information confidential which, in the case of staff, associates or consultants, shall be both during and after their current employment or engagement, on terms no less strict than this Agreement. Any disclosure of such Confidential Information by any staff, associate or consultant or Group Party will be deemed to be a breach of this Agreement by the Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'employees, contractors and advisers' to 'staff, associates and consultants' introduces ambiguity. While similar, these terms are not explicitly defined as equivalent. 'Contractors' have a specific legal meaning that 'associates' might lack. This could lead to disputes about the scope of the recipient's responsibility for breaches by these different categories of personnel. This creates a potential ambiguity about the scope of 'Permitted Personnel' defined in Section 3, introducing a contradiction. The contradiction occurs in Section 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party acknowledges and agrees: a. All Confidential Information acquired by either Party from the other Party shall be and shall remain the exclusive property of the disclosing Party or the member of its group providing it; b. To hold all Confidential Information in confidence and to procure that its representatives, advisers and associates hold all such Confidential Information in confidence. ANNEX 2 SAMPLE NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT I ELAN MOBILE MONEY ASSESSMENT AND CONTRACTING GUIDE 2 c. To limit access to such Confidential Information to authorised individuals (the “Permitted Personnel”) who have a need to know the Confidential Information in order for the Party to participate in the Matter. d. To procure that its Permitted Personnel are aware and adhere to the terms of this Agreement; e. To use such Confidential Information only for purposes of work, services or analysis related to the Matter and for other purposes only upon such terms as may be agreed upon between the Parties in writing; and f. To return promptly to the disclosing party, or to destroy any copies of such Confidential Information in written, graphic or other tangible form at that Party’s request and any such destruction shall be certified in writing to the disclosing Party by an authorised officer of the Recipient; g. The obligations of each of the Parties with respect to Confidential Information shall extend for a period of three (3) years following the date of disclosure of that Confidential Information; provided, however, that confidentiality obligations herein shall survive for a period of three (3) years from the date of termination of this Agreement; and h. Neither disclosure of Confidential Information, nor this Agreement shall be construed as (i) requiring a Party to disclose or accept Confidential Information, (ii) permitting a Party to use or sell the Confidential Information or products derived therefrom, or (iii) granting to either Party any rights by license or otherwise, express or implied, under any patent, copyright, trademark, trade secret or other intellectual property right now or hereafter obtained or licensable by the other Party, or (iv) a representation or warranty by either Party as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information (other than as expressly set out in any agreement subsequently entered into by the Parties).
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'employees, contractors and advisers', while the changed text uses 'staff, associates and consultants'. While similar, these terms are not explicitly equivalent. The explanation highlights that 'contractors' have a specific legal meaning that 'associates' might lack. This difference introduces ambiguity about the scope of 'Permitted Personnel' as defined in the Contradicted Text, Section 3, creating a contradiction. Section 3c of the contradicted text states that access to Confidential Information should be limited to 'Permitted Personnel', but the change introduces uncertainty about who qualifies as such, directly conflicting with this section and making it difficult to determine who the recipient is responsible for.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information acquired by either Party from the other Party shall be and shall remain the exclusive property of the disclosing Party or the member of its group providing it
perturbation.changed_text: All Sensitive Information acquired by either Party from the other Party shall be and shall remain the exclusive property of the disclosing Party or the member of its group providing it
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Confidential Information' with 'Sensitive Information' creates uncertainty. The contract defines 'Confidential Information' but not 'Sensitive Information'. It's unclear if 'Sensitive Information' is meant to be the same as 'Confidential Information' or something different, potentially weakening the protections afforded to truly confidential data. This pertubation is contradicting Section 15 where a clear definition of 'Confidential Information is explicitly mentioned. The contradiction is made in Section 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In this Agreement unless the context otherwise requires the following words and expressions shall have the following meanings: “Agreement” means this Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement; “Confidential Information” means any and all information and know-how that the Discloser and/or its Group Parties and/or its agents may from time to time disclose to the Recipient or the Recipient’s MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT ANNEX 2 SAMPLE NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT I ELAN MOBILE MONEY ASSESSMENT AND CONTRACTING GUIDE 3 Group Parties during the course of the Matter or which otherwise becomes known to the Recipient either during the course of the Matter or during the course of any previous discussions, negotiations or relationship with the Discloser in relation to the Matter, whether orally, in writing, digitally, in the form of machine readable code or embodied in hardware or any other physical medium which relates to the business, including but not limited to the business plan of the Discloser or their Group Parties, data, know-how, designs, illustrations, drawings, photographs, illustrations, notes, memoranda, terms of business, financial information, financial projections, financial records, customers and suppliers, sales and marketing information, spreadsheets, specifications, technical information and computer software of the Discloser and/or its Group Parties; “Intellectual Property Rights” means (i) patents, designs, trade marks and trade names (whether registered or unregistered), copyright and related rights, moral rights, database rights and know-how; (ii) all other intellectual property rights and similar or equivalent rights anywhere in the world which currently exist or are recognised in the future; and (iii) applications, extensions and renewals in relation to any such rights; and “Recipient” means a Party that has received Confidential Information of the other Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information', which is a defined term in the contract as stated in the perturbation explanation and exemplified in the 'Contradicted Text'. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Sensitive Information', which is NOT a defined term in the contract. This creates a contradiction because the contract now uses a term that has no defined meaning, making it unclear what information is actually protected and thus contradicting the explicit definition and protections afforded to 'Confidential Information' in the 'Contradicted Text'. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: To return promptly to the disclosing party, or to destroy any copies of such Confidential Information in written, graphic or other tangible form at that Party’s request and any such destruction shall be certified in writing to the disclosing Party by an authorised officer of the Recipient
perturbation.changed_text: To return promptly to the disclosing party, or to delete any copies of such Proprietary Data in written, graphic or other tangible form at that Party’s request and any such deletion shall be certified in writing to the disclosing Party by an authorised officer of the Recipient
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Data' and 'destroy' to 'delete' introduces ambiguity. 'Proprietary Data' is not defined, so its relationship to the defined 'Confidential Information' is unclear. 'Delete' might be interpreted differently than 'destroy,' particularly in the context of electronic data, potentially creating a loophole. This is contradicting the return of Confidential Information which the Recipient has, as the definition for it, is located in Section 15. The contradiction is made in Section 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In this Agreement unless the context otherwise requires the following words and expressions shall have the following meanings: “Agreement” means this Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement; “Confidential Information” means any and all information and know-how that the Discloser and/or its Group Parties and/or its agents may from time to time disclose to the Recipient or the Recipient’s MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT ANNEX 2 SAMPLE NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT I ELAN MOBILE MONEY ASSESSMENT AND CONTRACTING GUIDE 3 Group Parties during the course of the Matter or which otherwise becomes known to the Recipient either during the course of the Matter or during the course of any previous discussions, negotiations or relationship with the Discloser in relation to the Matter, whether orally, in writing, digitally, in the form of machine readable code or embodied in hardware or any other physical medium which relates to the business, including but not limited to the business plan of the Discloser or their Group Parties, data, know-how, designs, illustrations, drawings, photographs, illustrations, notes, memoranda, terms of business, financial information, financial projections, financial records, customers and suppliers, sales and marketing information, spreadsheets, specifications, technical information and computer software of the Discloser and/or its Group Parties; “Intellectual Property Rights” means (i) patents, designs, trade marks and trade names (whether registered or unregistered), copyright and related rights, moral rights, database rights and know-how; (ii) all other intellectual property rights and similar or equivalent rights anywhere in the world which currently exist or are recognised in the future; and (iii) applications, extensions and renewals in relation to any such rights; and “Recipient” means a Party that has received Confidential Information of the other Party.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Data' introduces a contradiction because 'Proprietary Data' is undefined, while 'Confidential Information' is explicitly defined in the contradicted text. This creates ambiguity about what information the recipient is obligated to return or delete, directly conflicting with the clear definition and obligations related to 'Confidential Information'. The instruction to 'delete' instead of 'destroy' further exacerbates the contradiction. This fits the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, as the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations. Therefore a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient acknowledges that money damages may not be an adequate remedy for any breach of this Agreement. The Discloser will be entitled to seek specific performance and injunctive and other equitable relief as a remedy for any breach of this Agreement in addition to all monetary remedies available at law and in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient acknowledges that monetary compensation may not be an adequate resolution for any violation of this Understanding. The Provider will be entitled to seek specific performance and injunctive and other equitable relief as a resolution for any violation of this Understanding in addition to all monetary remedies available at law and in equity.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Agreement' to 'Understanding', 'breach' to 'violation', 'remedy' to 'resolution' and 'Discloser' to 'Provider' introduces legal ambiguity. 'Understanding' implies a less formal arrangement than 'Agreement'. 'Violation' might be interpreted differently from 'breach' in legal proceedings. The change of Discloser to Provider will result in an internal contradiction as the term Provider is not defined in section 15, where is clearly defined the term Discloser and also Recipient. The contradiction is made in Section 5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In this Agreement unless the context otherwise requires the following words and expressions shall have the following meanings: “Agreement” means this Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement; “Confidential Information” means any and all information and know-how that the Discloser and/or its Group Parties and/or its agents may from time to time disclose to the Recipient or the Recipient’s MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT ANNEX 2 SAMPLE NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT I ELAN MOBILE MONEY ASSESSMENT AND CONTRACTING GUIDE 3 Group Parties during the course of the Matter or which otherwise becomes known to the Recipient either during the course of the Matter or during the course of any previous discussions, negotiations or relationship with the Discloser in relation to the Matter, whether orally, in writing, digitally, in the form of machine readable code or embodied in hardware or any other physical medium which relates to the business, including but not limited to the business plan of the Discloser or their Group Parties, data, know-how, designs, illustrations, drawings, photographs, illustrations, notes, memoranda, terms of business, financial information, financial projections, financial records, customers and suppliers, sales and marketing information, spreadsheets, specifications, technical information and computer software of the Discloser and/or its Group Parties; “Intellectual Property Rights” means (i) patents, designs, trade marks and trade names (whether registered or unregistered), copyright and related rights, moral rights, database rights and know-how; (ii) all other intellectual property rights and similar or equivalent rights anywhere in the world which currently exist or are recognised in the future; and (iii) applications, extensions and renewals in relation to any such rights; and “Recipient” means a Party that has received Confidential Information of the other Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies key terms like 'Agreement' to 'Understanding' and 'Discloser' to 'Provider'. The original text and contradicted text define 'Agreement' and 'Discloser'. The modified text's use of 'Understanding' introduces ambiguity as it is not defined, conflicting with the defined term 'Agreement'. Critically, the shift from 'Discloser' to 'Provider' creates a direct conflict because the contradicted text specifically defines 'Discloser' but does not define 'Provider'. This misaligned terminology causes an in-text contradiction based on the Perturbation Definition 3.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be amended only by written agreement executed by both Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Legal Document may be modified only by written legal document signed by both Sides.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Agreement' to 'Legal Document', 'amended' to 'modified', 'written agreement' to 'written legal document', 'executed' to 'signed' and 'Parties' to 'Sides' introduces ambiguity. Although the general meaning remains similar, the specific legal connotations of the original terms are lost. Whether a 'Legal Document' has the same enforceability as an 'Agreement' is questionable. This could lead to disagreements about the validity of modifications. This perturbation is contradicting the definition of Agreement in Section 15. The contradiction is made in Section 9.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In this Agreement unless the context otherwise requires the following words and expressions shall have the following meanings: “Agreement” means this Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement; “Confidential Information” means any and all information and know-how that the Discloser and/or its Group Parties and/or its agents may from time to time disclose to the Recipient or the Recipient’s MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT ANNEX 2 SAMPLE NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT I ELAN MOBILE MONEY ASSESSMENT AND CONTRACTING GUIDE 3 Group Parties during the course of the Matter or which otherwise becomes known to the Recipient either during the course of the Matter or during the course of any previous discussions, negotiations or relationship with the Discloser in relation to the Matter, whether orally, in writing, digitally, in the form of machine readable code or embodied in hardware or any other physical medium which relates to the business, including but not limited to the business plan of the Discloser or their Group Parties, data, know-how, designs, illustrations, drawings, photographs, illustrations, notes, memoranda, terms of business, financial information, financial projections, financial records, customers and suppliers, sales and marketing information, spreadsheets, specifications, technical information and computer software of the Discloser and/or its Group Parties; “Intellectual Property Rights” means (i) patents, designs, trade marks and trade names (whether registered or unregistered), copyright and related rights, moral rights, database rights and know-how; (ii) all other intellectual property rights and similar or equivalent rights anywhere in the world which currently exist or are recognised in the future; and (iii) applications, extensions and renewals in relation to any such rights; and “Recipient” means a Party that has received Confidential Information of the other Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces specific legal terms ('Agreement', 'amended', 'executed', 'Parties') with more generic terms ('Legal Document', 'modified', 'signed', 'Sides'). This creates a contradiction because the 'Agreement' is specifically defined in the contradicted text. By using 'Legal Document' instead, the changed text introduces ambiguity and undermines the defined meaning of 'Agreement', leading to a strong in-text contradiction and uncertainty about which terms apply and the validity of modifications.
